mappings.description: Create Container Request id
mappings.doc: Unique id for the container.

mappings.description: Reference to a Deployment using the container
mappings.doc: The deployment in which the container is used, so the device can send deployment events to Astarte for the create request

mappings.description: Container image id
mappings.doc: The id of the image to use when creating the container.

mappings.description: Container network ids
mappings.doc: The ids of the network to connect with the container.

mappings.description: Container volume ids
mappings.doc: The ids of the volumes to mount on the container.

mappings.description: Container device mappings ids
mappings.doc: The ids of the device mappings to mount on the container.

mappings.description: Container device request ids
mappings.doc: The ids of the devices to request for the container.

mappings.description: Container hostname
mappings.doc: The hostname to use for the container, as a valid RFC 1123 hostname.

mappings.description: Container hostname
mappings.doc: The domain name to use for the container.

mappings.description: Run as this user inside the container
mappings.doc: Can be either user-name or UID, and optional group-name or GID, separated by colon (`<user-name|UID>[<:group-name|GID>]`).

mappings.description: Command to run specified as array of strings
mappings.doc: This will be passed to the entrypoint

mappings.description: A test to perform to check that the container is healthy.
mappings.doc: A non-zero exit code indicates a failed healthcheck

mappings.description: The time to wait before considering the check to have hung.
mappings.doc: It should be 0 or at least 1000000 (1 ms). 0 means inherit.

mappings.description: The time to wait between checks in nanoseconds.
mappings.doc: It should be 0 or at least 1000000 (1 ms). 0 means inherit.

mappings.description: The number of consecutive failures needed to consider a container as unhealthy.
mappings.doc: 0 means inherit.

mappings.description: Start period for the container to initialize before starting health-retries countdown in nanoseconds.
mappings.doc: It should be 0 or at least 1000000 (1 ms). 0 means inherit.

mappings.description: The time to wait between checks in nanoseconds during the start period.
mappings.doc: It should be 0 or at least 1000000 (1 ms). 0 means inherit.

mappings.description: The working directory for commands to run in.
The entry point for the container.
Disable networking for the container.
User-defined key/value metadata.
mappings.doc: This is a list of the keys for the values in labelsValues.

mappings.description: User-defined key/value metadata.
mappings.doc: This is a list of the values for the keys in labelsKeys.

mappings.description: Signal to stop a container
Timeout to stop a container in seconds.
mappings.doc: Default to 10 seconds.

mappings.description: Container restart policy
mappings.doc: The behavior to apply when the container exits. Possible values are: ['', 'no', 'always' 'unless-stopped', 'on-failure']

mappings.description: If on-failure is used, the number of times to retry before giving up.
Container environment
mappings.doc: Array of key=value environment variables. The key cannot contain `=`.

mappings.description: Container binds
mappings.doc: A list of volume bindings for this container. Each volume binding is a string in one of these forms: host-src:container-dest[:options], or volume-name:container-dest[:options]. The container-dest must be an absolute path.

mappings.description: Network mode used for this container.
mappings.doc: Supported standard values are: bridge, host, none, and container:<name|id>. Any other value is taken as a custom network's name to which this container should connect to. If you are using a different container engine than docker, there could be other values.

mappings.description: Container port bindings
mappings.doc: Array of ip:[host_port:]container_port[/protocol] | [hostPort:]containerPort[/protocol]. Protocol is TCP by default

mappings.description: Container exposed ports
mappings.doc: Array of ~<port>/<tcp|udp|sctp>`

mappings.description: List of hostname/IP to add to the container's /etc/hosts
mappings.doc: A list of hostnames/IP mappings to add to the container's /etc/hosts file. Specified in the form ["hostname:IP"]. You can use the `host-gateway` to connect to the host IP

mappings.description: A list of kernel capabilities to add to the container.
mappings.doc: A list of kernel capabilities to add to the container.

mappings.description: A list of kernel capabilities to drop from the container.
mappings.doc: A list of kernel capabilities to drop from the container.

mappings.description: An integer value representing this container's relative CPU weight versus other containers.
CPUs in which to allow execution (e.g., 0-3, 0,1).
The length of a CPU period in microseconds.
Microseconds of CPU time that the container can get in a CPU period.
The length of a CPU real-time period in microseconds.
mappings.doc: The length of a CPU real-time period in microseconds. Set to 0 to allocate no time allocated to real-time tasks.

mappings.description: The length of a CPU real-time runtime in microseconds.
mappings.doc: The length of a CPU real-time runtime in microseconds. Set to 0 to allocate no time allocated to real-time tasks.

mappings.description: Memory limit in bytes.
mappings.doc: Memory limit in bytes. Default to 0 for unlimited.

mappings.description: Memory soft limit in bytes. Default to 0 for unlimited.
Total memory limit (memory + swap).
mappings.doc: Total memory limit (memory + swap). Set as -1 to enable unlimited swap.

mappings.description: Memory swappiness
mappings.doc: Tune a container's memory swappiness behavior. Accepts an integer between 0 and 100.

mappings.description: A list of cgroup rules to apply to the container
A list of resource limits to set in the container.
mappings.doc: List of names for the Ulimits objects mapped to the name, soft and hard fields.

mappings.description: A list of resource limits to set in the container.
mappings.doc: List of soft limits for the Ulimits objects mapped to the name, soft and hard fields.

mappings.description: A list of resource limits to set in the container.
mappings.doc: List of hard limits for the Ulimits objects mapped to the name, soft and hard fields.

mappings.description: Automatically remove the container when the container's process exits.
mappings.doc: This has no effect if RestartPolicy is set.

mappings.description: Driver that this container uses to mount volumes.
Storage driver options for this container.
mappings.doc: Key for the values in storageOptValues

mappings.description: Storage driver options for this container.
mappings.doc: Values for storageOptKey

mappings.description: Mount the container's root filesystem as read only.
A map of container directories which should be replaced by tmpfs mounts.
mappings.doc: The key of a map of container directories and options which should be replaced by tmpfs mounts, and their corresponding mount options. For example: '/run'

mappings.description: A map of container directories which should be replaced by tmpfs mounts.
mappings.doc: The value of a map of container directories and options which should be replaced by tmpfs mounts, and their corresponding mount options. For example: 'rw,noexec,nosuid,size=65536k'

mappings.description: cgroup namespace mode for the container.
mappings.doc: Possible values are: `private` the container runs in its own private cgroup namespace, `host` use the host system's cgroup namespace

mappings.description: A list of DNS servers for the container to use.
A list of DNS options.
A list of DNS search domains.
A list of additional groups that the container process will run as.
IPC sharing mode for the container.
An integer value containing the score given to the container in order to tune OOM killer preferences.
Sets the usernamespace mode for the container when usernamespace remapping option is enabled.
A list of kernel parameters (sysctls) to set in the container.
A list of kernel parameters (sysctls) to set in the container.
Size of /dev/shm in bytes.
mappings.doc: Must be >= 0. If omitted, the system uses 64MB.

mappings.description: Runtime to use with this container.
Run privileged
mappings.doc: Runs the exec process with extended privileges.

mappings.description: The logging configuration for this container
mappings.doc: Name of the logging driver used for the container or 'none' if logging is disabled.

mappings.description: Driver-specific configuration options for the logging driver.
Driver-specific configuration options for the logging driver.
Block IO weight (relative weight).
mappings.doc: Value from [0..1000]

mappings.description: Block IO weight (relative device weight).
mappings.doc: This value is related to the blkioWeightDeviceWeight value

mappings.description: Block IO weight (relative device weight).
mappings.doc: This value is related to the blkioWeightDevicePath

mappings.description: Limit read rate (bytes per second) to a device.
mappings.doc: This value is related to the blkioDeviceReadBpsRate value

mappings.description: Limit read rate (bytes per second) to a device.
mappings.doc: This value is related to the blkioDeviceReadBpsPath

mappings.description: Limit write rate (bytes per second) to a device.
mappings.doc: This value is related to the blkioDeviceWriteBpsRate value

mappings.description: Limit write rate (bytes per second) to a device.
mappings.doc: This value is related to the blkioDeviceWriteBpsPath

mappings.description: Limit read rate (IO per second) to a device.
mappings.doc: This value is related to the blkioDeviceReadIopsRate value

mappings.description: Limit read rate (IO per second) to a device.
mappings.doc: This value is related to the blkioDeviceReadIopsPath

mappings.description: Limit write rate (bytes per second) to a device.
mappings.doc: This value is related to the blkioDeviceWriteIopsRate value

mappings.description: Limit write rate (bytes per second) to a device.
mappings.doc: This value is related to the blkioDeviceWriteIopsPath

mappings.description: a list of string values to customize labels for mls systems, such as selinux.
Set the PID (Process) Namespace mode for the container.
mappings.doc: It can be either: `container:<name|id>` joins another container's PID namespace, `host` use the host's PID namespace inside the container

mappings.description: The list of paths to be masked inside the container (this overrides the default set of paths).
The list of paths to be set as read-only inside the container (this overrides the default set of paths).
